content:
# Par Level Calculation Worksheet

**Tool ID:** T095-01
**Block:** B095 (Par-Level Stock Management)
**Domain:** Operations & Workflow (D05_operations_workflow)
**Level:** L1
**Format:** Template
**Role:** Manager
**Time Required:** 10–20 minutes
**Version:** 1.0

---
## Welcome

Running out of oat milk at 09:30 on a Saturday does not just cost one sale — it costs every customer who sees the apology sign and walks out. This worksheet turns guesswork ordering into a calculated system that prevents stockouts and stops over-ordering from eating your margins.

A ready-to-use template that removes the guesswork. Fill it in once, review weekly, and never panic-order from the cash-and-carry again.

**Built for Manager working in Operations & Workflow.**
Use it. Adapt it. Make it yours.

---

## Purpose

A structured worksheet for calculating par levels for every stock item based on average daily usage, delivery frequency, lead time, and safety buffer. Produces a definitive minimum-stock number for each ingredient and consumable so that reordering becomes automatic rather than reactive.

This tool supports **Par-Level Stock Management** (B095) and is designed to be practical, printable, and immediately useful in a working cafe environment.

---

## How to Use This Tool

**Who:** Manager
**Time Required:** 10–20 minutes per category (first use); 5–10 minutes for weekly review
**Frequency:** Complete fully on first use. Review and adjust weekly, or when menu/supplier changes occur.

**What You'll Need:**
- This document (printed or on tablet)
- POS sales data or till reports for the past 2–4 weeks
- Supplier delivery schedules and lead times
- Current stock counts
- Pen or device for notes

**Steps:**
1. Gather 2–4 weeks of sales data from POS (or manual counts if POS unavailable)
2. List every stock item using the category tables below
3. Calculate Average Daily Usage (ADU) for each item
4. Record delivery frequency and lead time for each supplier
5. Apply the par level formula: **Par = (ADU x Lead Time in Days) + Safety Buffer**
6. Enter the calculated par level in the worksheet
7. Transfer final par levels to shelf labels (see T095-03)
8. Schedule weekly review to adjust based on actual usage

---

## Template

Complete all sections below. Use actual sales data — estimates are acceptable for Week 1 only.

### Section 1: Overview

| Field | Details |
|-------|---------|
| Date completed | *e.g., 15/03/2026* |
| Completed by | *e.g., Jonas (Manager)* |
| Venue / Location | *e.g., Cafe 36, Vilnius Old Town* |
| Data period used | *e.g., 01/03–14/03/2026 (14 days)* |
| Next scheduled review | *e.g., 22/03/2026* |

### Section 2: Coffee & Tea

| Item | Unit | Avg Daily Usage | Delivery Freq (days) | Lead Time (days) | Safety Buffer | **Calculated Par** | Current Stock | Order Needed? |
|------|------|----------------|---------------------|------------------|---------------|-------------------|---------------|---------------|
| Espresso beans (house) | kg | *e.g., 2.5* | *3* | *1* | *1 day (2.5 kg)* | *10.0 kg* | | |
| Espresso beans (guest/single origin) | kg | *e.g., 0.8* | *7* | *2* | *1 day (0.8 kg)* | *7.2 kg* | | |
| Decaf beans | kg | *e.g., 0.4* | *7* | *2* | *1 day (0.4 kg)* | *3.6 kg* | | |
| Loose leaf tea (per variety) | g | *e.g., 50* | *14* | *3* | *2 days (100 g)* | *950 g* | | |
| Tea bags (per variety) | pcs | | | | | | | |
| Hot chocolate powder | kg | | | | | | | |
| Syrups (per flavour) | bottles | *e.g., 0.3* | *14* | *3* | *1 (bottle)* | *6 bottles* | | |
| Matcha powder | g | | | | | | | |

### Section 3: Dairy & Alternatives

| Item | Unit | Avg Daily Usage | Delivery Freq (days) | Lead Time (days) | Safety Buffer | **Calculated Par** | Current Stock | Order Needed? |
|------|------|----------------|---------------------|------------------|---------------|-------------------|---------------|---------------|
| Whole milk | litres | *e.g., 18* | *3 (Mon/Wed/Fri)* | *1* | *1 day (18 L)* | *54 L* | | |
| Oat milk | litres | *e.g., 12* | *3* | *1* | *1 day (12 L)* | *36 L* | | |
| Soy milk | litres | *e.g., 3* | *7* | *2* | *1 day (3 L)* | *24 L* | | |
| Almond milk | litres | | | | | | | |
| Cream / whipping cream | litres | | | | | | | |
| Butter | kg | | | | | | | |

### Section 4: Food Ingredients

| Item | Unit | Avg Daily Usage | Delivery Freq (days) | Lead Time (days) | Safety Buffer | **Calculated Par** | Current Stock | Order Needed? |
|------|------|----------------|---------------------|------------------|---------------|-------------------|---------------|---------------|
| Sourdough bread | loaves | *e.g., 8* | *1 (daily)* | *1* | *2 loaves* | *10 loaves* | | |
| Avocados | pcs | *e.g., 12* | *3* | *1* | *4 pcs* | *16 pcs* | | |
| Eggs (free range) | pcs | *e.g., 36* | *3* | *1* | *12 pcs* | *48 pcs* | | |
| Salad leaves (mixed) | kg | | | | | | | |
| Smoked salmon | kg | | | | | | | |
| Bacon | kg | | | | | | | |
| Cheese (per variety) | kg | | | | | | | |
| Tomatoes | kg | | | | | | | |

### Section 5: Pastries & Baked Goods

| Item | Unit | Avg Daily Usage | Delivery Freq (days) | Lead Time (days) | Safety Buffer | **Calculated Par** | Current Stock | Order Needed? |
|------|------|----------------|---------------------|------------------|---------------|-------------------|---------------|---------------|
| Croissants (plain) | pcs | *e.g., 15* | *1 (daily)* | *1* | *3 pcs* | *18 pcs* | | |
| Pain au chocolat | pcs | | | | | | | |
| Muffins (per variety) | pcs | | | | | | | |
| Cinnamon rolls | pcs | | | | | | | |
| Cakes / slices (display) | pcs | | | | | | | |

### Section 6: Packaging & Consumables

| Item | Unit | Avg Daily Usage | Delivery Freq (days) | Lead Time (days) | Safety Buffer | **Calculated Par** | Current Stock | Order Needed? |
|------|------|----------------|---------------------|------------------|---------------|-------------------|---------------|---------------|
| Takeaway cups 8oz | pcs | *e.g., 45* | *14* | *3* | *2 days (90)* | *855 pcs* | | |
| Takeaway cups 12oz | pcs | *e.g., 60* | *14* | *3* | *2 days (120)* | *1140 pcs* | | |
| Lids (flat/dome) | pcs | | | | | | | |
| Paper bags (small/large) | pcs | | | | | | | |
| Napkins | pcs | | | | | | | |
| Deli boxes / food containers | pcs | | | | | | | |

### Section 7: Cleaning & Sundries

| Item | Unit | Avg Daily Usage | Delivery Freq (days) | Lead Time (days) | Safety Buffer | **Calculated Par** | Current Stock | Order Needed? |
|------|------|----------------|---------------------|------------------|---------------|-------------------|---------------|---------------|
| Sanitiser spray | bottles | *e.g., 0.5* | *14* | *3* | *2 bottles* | *11 bottles* | | |
| Blue roll / paper towels | rolls | | | | | | | |
| Bin bags | pcs | | | | | | | |
| Dishwasher tablets/liquid | units | | | | | | | |
| Milk jug cleaner / Cafiza | bottles | | | | | | | |

### Section 8: Par Level Formula Reference

**Standard Formula:**

> **Par Level = (Average Daily Usage x Lead Time in Days) + Safety Buffer**

**Safety Buffer Guidelines:**

| Item Criticality | Buffer Rule | Example |
|-----------------|------------|---------|
| Critical (milk, beans, cups) | 1 full day of usage | ADU 18L milk = 18L buffer |
| Important (bread, pastries) | 50% of daily usage | ADU 8 loaves = 4 loaf buffer |
| Non-critical (napkins, stirrers) | 2 days of usage | Allows for delivery delays |
| Perishable with short shelf life | 0.5 day of usage | Minimise waste risk |

**Seasonal Adjustment Notes:**

| Season | Items to Adjust | Typical Change |
|--------|----------------|----------------|
| Summer (Jun–Aug) | Iced drinks, cold brew, ice, salads | +25–40% |
| Winter (Nov–Feb) | Hot chocolate, soup, heavier bakes | +15–25% |
| Event/holiday weeks | All items | Review forecast individually |

### Section 9: Sign-Off

| Role | Name | Signature | Date |
|------|------|-----------|------|
| Manager | | | |
| Owner (if reviewing) | | | |

---

## Common Risks

### Risk 1: Using Estimates Instead of Data

**Why it happens:** POS data seems hard to pull, or the cafe has no digital records.
**How to prevent:** Even 5 days of manual tally counting (hash marks on a clipboard) gives you usable ADU numbers. Do not skip this step.
**If it's already happening:** Run a one-week stock audit — count opening stock, count closing stock, the difference is usage. Adjust par levels immediately.

### Risk 2: Par Levels Calculated Once and Never Reviewed

**Why it happens:** The initial setup feels complete. Menu changes, seasonal shifts, and supplier changes silently invalidate old numbers.
**How to prevent:** Put a recurring calendar reminder every Monday to review top-10 items by cost. Full review monthly.
**If it's already happening:** Start with your three highest-cost categories (coffee, milk, pastries) and recalculate today.

### Risk 3: Ignoring Lead Time Changes

**Why it happens:** Supplier promises next-day but actually delivers in 2 days during busy periods.
**How to prevent:** Track actual delivery arrival dates for 4 weeks. Use real lead time, not promised lead time, in your formula.
**If it's already happening:** Add one extra day to all lead times as a buffer until you have reliable data.

---

## Customisation Notes

**This tool is a starting point, not a rulebook.**

**Independent / Specialty Cafe:** Add rows for guest espressos, seasonal single-origins, and specialty ingredients (e.g., house-made syrups, cold brew concentrate). Your item list will be longer but your volumes per item will be lower.
**Chain / Franchise:** Align item names with corporate procurement codes. Par levels may be centrally dictated — use this worksheet to validate whether central pars match your actual usage.
**Kiosk / Mobile:** Reduce to essentials — coffee, milk, cups, lids, a few food items. Par levels are tighter because storage is limited.
**Bakery Cafe:** Add categories for flour, yeast, proving agents, display rotation items. Par levels for baked goods are time-sensitive — factor in bake schedule, not just daily usage.

**Making it yours:**
- Add or remove rows to match your actual menu and supply list
- Adjust safety buffer percentages based on your supplier reliability
- Add a column for supplier name and contact if you want a single reference document
- Colour-code by supplier to make ordering faster

The structure matters more than the specific fields. Keep the structure, change the content.

---

## Connected Resources

**Other tools in this block:**
- T095-02 — Weekly Order Template (L2, template)
- T095-03 — Par Level Shelf Labels (L1, checklist)
- T095-04 — Monthly Par Level Review Report (L3, dashboard)

**Related blocks:**

**Parent block:** B095 — Par-Level Stock Management
**Module:** M05: Service Flow & Peak Management
---

*Tool Version 1.0 — OIS Cafe v2*
*Built for cafe operators who value clarity, consistency, and craft.*
